# Confidential Computing Deep Dive

> A comprehensive guide to Confidential Computing, TEEs, attestation,
> Confidential VMs, and CNCF Confidential Containers (CoCo).
> Author: Pradipta Banerjee, Project Maintainer — Confidential Containers

## Chapters

- [Introduction](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/intro.md): This book is a comprehensive deep dive into Confidential Computing. It covers the theory, architecture, components, and use cases to understand and work with confidential computing.
- [The Trust Boundary Problem](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/trust-boundary.md): Cloud computing promises flexibility and scale. But it comes at a cost: when you run workloads on someone else's infrastructure, you must trust the infrastructure owner. Let's examine what that trust 
- [What is Confidential Computing?](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/overview.md): Modern data security is often described using three pillars:
- [Use Cases](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/use-cases.md): Here are the primary use cases of confidential computing.
- [Building Blocks](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/building-blocks.md): Before diving into Confidential Containers, it helps to understand the foundational concepts and technologies that make confidential computing possible.
- [Remote Attestation](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/remote-attestation.md): Confidential computing has two dimensions: runtime isolation and attestation.
- [Three Pillars of Confidential Computing](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/three-pillars.md): Confidential Computing in cloud-native environments is organized into three deployment tiers, each building on the previous one.
- [Confidential Virtual Machine (CVM)](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/confidential-vms.md): A Confidential Virtual Machine (CVM) is a virtual machine that runs inside a hardware Trusted Execution Environment (TEE), protecting the guest's memory and execution from inspection or tampering by t
- [Confidential Containers (CoCo)](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/confidential-containers.md): Confidential Containers — a generic term for containers deployed inside Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs).
- [Confidential Cluster](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/confidential-clusters.md): A Confidential Cluster is a Kubernetes cluster where Confidential Virtual Machines (CVMs) serve as Kubernetes nodes — both worker nodes (running workloads) and, in a full deployment, control plane nod
- [Trustee](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/trustee.md): [Trustee](https://github.com/confidential-containers/trustee) is the remote attestation solution for confidential containers and confidential VMs.
- [Hands-On Labs](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/labs.md): This chapter provides practical exercises to complement the concepts covered in previous chapters. The labs are designed to be independent — you can work through them in order or jump to the one most 
- [Lab 1: CVM Attestation on Azure](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/lab1-cvm-attestation.md): This lab demonstrates the core attestation loop on a real Confidential VM. You will provision two Azure VMs — a standard Ubuntu VM hosting Trustee (KBS) and a hardware-backed Confidential VM running t
- [Lab 2: CoCo Without Confidential Hardware](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/lab2-coco-without-hw.md): This lab runs the full Confidential Containers stack on any Linux VM — no TEE required. A sample verifier replaces hardware attestation, letting you exercise VM isolation, guest-pull images, and secre
- [Lab 3: CoCo-fy a Workload with cococtl](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/lab3-cococtl.md): `cococtl` (`kubectl coco`) automates the steps from Lab 2: it sets the runtime class, converts Kubernetes Secrets to sealed secrets, generates the initdata annotation (which configures the KBS URL and
- [Lab 4: CoCo on a Real CVM via Peer-Pods (BYOM)](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/lab4-byom.md): This lab runs a Kubernetes cluster on a standard VM and connects it to an Azure Confidential VM as a peer pod. Workload containers execute inside the hardware-backed SEV-SNP CVM, not on the K8s node. 
- [References](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/chapters/references.md): For a comprehensive list of Confidential Computing resources, tools, papers, and projects:

## Full book (single file)

- [llms-full.txt](https://cc-book.github.io/cc-book/llms-full.txt): All chapters concatenated into one document.
